commands.repair_reason: Maintainer should decide whether to land this focused recovery change with the documented false-abort risk; there is no narrow automated repair to make.; Cleared: No dependency, workflow, secret, permission, or external code-execution changes were found; the risk is operational recovery behavior, not a security regression. (sha=e9ad954dac33c0d6842a223f64f5b244423f471c)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a stale-progress liveness gate to diagnostic stuck-session recovery, threads the resolved stuck-session abort threshold into recovery requests, and adds regression tests for leaked active-run handles with queued work.

Reproducibility: yes. from source and logs, though I did not execute a failing current-main run. Current main can classify queued work as stale_session_state and then skip recovery with active_reply_work if the embedded-run registry still reports active work; the PR body also includes before-fix failing test output and after-fix real-component output.
commands.review_followup: Maintainer should decide whether to land this focused recovery change with the documented false-abort risk; there is no narrow automated repair to make.
